paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Gwendoline (1882-1951) and Margaret (1884-1963) Davies were the granddaughters of David Davies, a Welshman who amassed a fortune in the shipping and mining industries. In 1907, when Gwendoline came into her inheritance (Margaret would follow in 1909), the sisters were said to be the wealthiest unmarried women in Britain. Their religious upbringing in rural Wales gave them a deep sense of social responsibility and they chose to use their inheritance for cultural and philanthropic* purposes.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: While there was no real family history of art collecting, the sisters' education was rigorously geared toward such pursuits. Their London school focused on cultural rather than academic study, and they travelled extensively with their governess, Jane Blaker, visiting art galleries and making extensive notes on the collections there.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: The sisters began to make regular art purchases from 1908, which roughly coincided with the dates of their inheritance. They took advice from various people, including the art dealer Hugh Blaker (the brother of Jane Blaker) and David Croal Thompson, who was also an art dealer. While it was long assumed that these men were largely responsible for the nature of the sisters' collection, it has recently been accepted that Gwendoline and Margaret retained a far more active role in the process.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The sisters' journals reveal their preference for Old Master** paintings. Yet they initially made very few attempts to secure any such works. While the sisters were wealthy in relative terms, their income was nothing compared to the fortunes of American art collectors of this period, such as J. Pierpont Morgan. Quite simply, high quality Old Master works were, if not beyond their means, then beyond what they were willing to pay for them. Instead, their early purchases were of the fashionable, safe variety, and included, for example, paintings by the French artist Jean-Baptiste-Camille Corot.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: However, it was only a few years before their collecting took a new direction and they turned to the work of the French Impressionists***. We know that Hugh Blaker, as a champion of contemporary French art had a hand in the decision, and we know also that they would have seen examples on their various trips to Paris. Whatever the precise reason for this change, their first purchases of Impressionist art, made in October 1912, were scenes of Venice by the French artist Claude Monet. Over the next 12 years, the sisters amassed the bulk of their Impressionist collection, including six further works by Monet, two more by Manet, and three by Renoir, including his well-known painting La Parisienne.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: The First World War (1914-1918) played a part in the development of the sisters' collection. Their initial response to the war effort was to finance the safe passage of artists from occupied Belgium to Wales, as a humanitarian act, but also with the hope of establishing a vibrant artists' community in the area. Later in the conflict, both sisters decided to volunteer at a canteen for troops at Troyes, in northern France; Gwendoline in 1916, followed by Margaret in 1917.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: It was tedious and distressing work, which would have a permanent effect on Gwendoline's health. Yet on one of numerous trips to nearby Paris, she visited the Bernheim-Jeune Gallery. Here she acquired two works by Cezanne - Provencal Landscape and The Francois Zola Dam. The paintings were shipped directly to Bath, England, where they became the first works by Cezanne to go on display in a public gallery in Britain.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Commentators have often described the sisters as unlikely pioneer collectors. Much is made of their isolation in rural Wales and the fact that they didn't make friends with artists or gallery owners. Yet they didn't feel obliged to follow fashionable tastes and were free to pursue their own preferences. Although they relied on a trusted circle of advisers, they made frequent trips to London and Paris, and also regularly had paintings sent to their home for consideration.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: By the early 1920s, Gwendoline felt increasingly uncomfortable buying art works when faced with the poverty and social upheaval created by the First World War. Her philanthropic pursuits then became focused almost exclusively on social causes and the development of the sisters' home at Gregynog Hall into a conference center and venue for the Gregynog Festival of Music and Poetry. Gwendoline made her final art purchase in March 1926. Margaret also stopped collecting around this time, but started again in the 1930s acquiring, on a relatively small scale, work by contemporary British artists.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: The sisters collected French Impressionist paintings at a time when such art was routinely ignored by individuals and institutions alike. The Gwendoline and Margaret Davies collection, donated in 1951 and 1963 respectively to the National Museum Wales, contains major examples of work by leading French Impressionists. In collecting paintings that they loved, the sisters created a lasting and meaningful cultural legacy for the people of Wales and beyond.

paragraphs.label: K
paragraphs.text:
* philanthropic: seeking to promote the welfare of others, often by charitable funding
** Old Master: a highly respected artist of great skill who worked in Europe before about 1800
*** Impressionist: an artist with a style of painting that developed in France in the late 1800s

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Humans are finely attuned to noise, and for good reason. From an evolutionary perspective, sounds give us vital information, helping us navigate the world and avoid danger. To help ensure loud or unexpected noises get the attention they deserve, our internal chemistry alters in response to them. Our blood pressure goes up, muscles tense and glands release hormones that prepare us for fight or flight. In the short term, this is a good thing. When we are exposed to too much noise over the long term, however, those responses can lead to a multitude of health issues, from sleep disturbance to even cardiovascular disease.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: The World Health Organization has recently designated excessive noise as an underestimated threat to public health, and has said that people living in cities such as Mumbai, Tokyo and Buenos Aires are being exposed to far more than the recommended 40 decibels of noise at night. A report from the European Environment Agency concluded that noise was an ongoing and widespread issue in Europe, with at least 1 in 5 people consistently exposed to levels considered harmful to health. There are no earlids that can protect your brain from noise, says Nick Antonio, an acoustic consultant who has contributed to the British and international standards for noise.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: The good news is that several cities have been working to turn the volume down. One of the first to do so was London. 'By providing recommendations for quieter buses, reducing noise from roads and also controlling noise from aircraft, they were able to make the city quieter', says Antonio. Other cities have introduced noise-reducing road coatings, for instance, alongside greenery that muffles sound. Some solutions are more specific: Washington DC's ban on petrol-powered leaf blowers came into effect recently, while in New York City, legislation has been approved to fine people who modify their vehicles to make them noisier. People are seeing the benefits of these more quiet environments in their cities, Antonio says. I expect we will see much more of this in the future.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Researchers are also seeking to understand what aspects of silent experiences are most beneficial to our health. One of the best-researched is the flotation tank: a lightproof, soundproof tank of salt water in which a person floats as a form of deep relaxation. While some people experience altered perception in the tanks, involving subtle humming sounds and visual effects, these effects are benign and do not detract from the benefits of the experience, says Justin Feinstein, a clinical neuropsychologist. When you do not have external sensory stimuli coming in, the brain tries to fill the void to make sense of this dark and silent world, he explains. In these tanks, some people can even hear the sound of their eyes blinking, says Feinstein. But it is the ability to focus on the breath that helps people reach a relaxed or meditative state.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: To further explore flotation tanks as a therapeutic tool, Feinstein and his colleagues recruited 50 people with a variety of conditions related to stress and had them answer a questionnaire prior to and following a flotation session. Participants reported decreases in muscle tension, pain and symptoms of their conditions after a single, 1-hour float, alongside an increase in feelings of relaxation and overall wellbeing. Less is known about what effects sensory deprivation can have on the brain. To investigate, Feinstein's team had 48 people participate in either three 90-minute float sessions or three 90-minute periods of relaxing on a chair which reclined. Participants had their brains scanned using functional magnetic resonance imaging at the beginning and end of the trial. Float sessions uniquely decreased activity in the default mode network (DMN), a collection of brain regions commonly linked with depression. Feinstein says it is an exciting finding, because flotation tanks seem to offer a way of resetting our nervous system to prevent it from getting out of balance.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Neurobiologist Tai Dotan Ben-Soussan is also an advocate of silence as a therapy. 'When we find ways to be quiet, we are not only quiet in our environment, but quiet in our inner selves', she explains. This allows us to be more aware of what is happening around us and what the situation may require from us so we can provide a more adequate response. Not everyone will benefit from silence to the same extent, but Ben-Soussan says one characteristic is key: the person must need to want to engage in the experience. We see from animal models and human studies that volition and intentionality is important, she says. When people do not want silence, it can be very distressing.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Eric Pfeifer, a psychotherapy researcher, also concedes that some people may not benefit from silence, particularly those who are in a heightened state of stress. People in these states may not be able to relax or calm down in a silent condition, he says. Professional guidance can be useful, he adds, allowing people to approach silence slowly so that they can gradually enjoy the benefits. And Pfeifer is convinced that silence is more attainable in everyday life than people think. First, complete silence is not necessary. In a recent study, he found that participants reported more relaxation and less boredom when they sat quietly in an outdoor garden compared with a completely silent room. Second, Pfeifer believes we do not need a lot of silence to gain benefits. You do not need to spend hours in silence, he says. It is likely better to have more frequency of silence for a few minutes at a time than a longer period of silence only once a week. Just finding those places in your daily life where you can find some silence can make a big difference.

groups.desc:
Questions 14-17
Reading Passage 2 has seven sections, A-G.
Which section contains the following information?
Write the correct letter, A-G, in boxes 14-17 on your answer sheet.

questions.title: examples of strategies to decrease the noise that the public are exposed to
questions.analysis: 题干要求找出‘减少公众所受噪音的策略’的例子。C段明确列举了多项具体措施，如伦敦推广更安静的公交车、使用降噪道路涂层、种植吸音绿植，以及华盛顿特区禁止汽油动力吹叶机和纽约市对改装车辆罚款等。这些内容与题干中的‘strategies to decrease the noise’完全对应，因此答案为C。

central_sentences.analysis: C段列举多项降噪措施，如 quieter buses、road coatings 等。
sentences: By providing recommendations for quieter buses, reducing noise from roads and also controlling noise from aircraft, they were able to make the city quieter, says Antonio.
Other cities have introduced noise-reducing road coatings, for instance, alongside greenery that muffles sound.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: strategies to decrease noise -> quieter buses, noise-reducing road coatings, greenery, bans, legislation

central_sentences.sentence: By providing recommendations for quieter buses, reducing noise from roads and also controlling noise from aircraft, they were able to make the city quieter, says Antonio.
Other cities have introduced noise-reducing road coatings, for instance, alongside greenery that muffles sound.

questions.title: data indicating the extent of the problem of excessive noise
questions.analysis: 题干询问‘有关过度噪音问题严重程度的数据’。B段引用世界卫生组织（WHO）和欧洲环境署（EEA）的报告，指出孟买、东京等城市夜间噪音远超40分贝推荐值，并提到欧洲至少五分之一人口长期暴露于有害噪音水平。这些具体数据直接说明了噪音问题的广泛性和严重性，因此答案为B。

central_sentences.analysis: B段引用WHO和EEA数据说明噪音问题的普遍性与严重性。
sentences: The World Health Organization has recently designated excessive noise as an underestimated threat to public health, and has said that people living in cities such as Mumbai, Tokyo and Buenos Aires are being exposed to far more than the recommended 40 decibels of noise at night.

A report from the European Environment Agency concluded that noise was an ongoing and widespread issue in Europe, with at least 1 in 5 people consistently exposed to levels considered harmful to health.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: data indicating extent -> 1 in 5 people, far more than 40 decibels

central_sentences.sentence: The World Health Organization has recently designated excessive noise as an underestimated threat to public health, and has said that people living in cities such as Mumbai, Tokyo and Buenos Aires are being exposed to far more than the recommended 40 decibels of noise at night.

A report from the European Environment Agency concluded that noise was an ongoing and widespread issue in Europe, with at least 1 in 5 people consistently exposed to levels considered harmful to health.

questions.title: a description of physiological changes in our bodies when we hear sudden noises
questions.analysis: 题干要求找出‘听到突发噪音时身体发生的生理变化’的描述。A段明确指出，面对响亮或意外噪音时，人体会出现血压升高、肌肉紧张以及腺体释放应激激素等反应，这些都属于典型的生理变化，且与‘fight or flight’机制相关。该描述完全匹配题干要求，因此答案为A。

central_sentences.analysis: A段描述突发噪音引发的生理反应：血压升高、肌肉紧张等。
sentences: Our blood pressure goes up, muscles tense and glands release hormones that prepare us for fight or flight.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: physiological changes -> blood pressure up, muscles tense, hormones release

central_sentences.sentence: Our blood pressure goes up, muscles tense and glands release hormones that prepare us for fight or flight.

questions.title: evidence that a relatively quiet environment can be more beneficial than a totally silent one
questions.analysis: 题干提到‘相对安静的环境可能比完全寂静更有益’。G段中Eric Pfeifer的研究发现，参与者在户外花园安静坐着时，比在完全无声的房间中感到更放松、更少无聊。这表明一定程度的环境声音（如自然声）反而优于绝对静音，与题干表述一致，因此答案为G。

central_sentences.analysis: G段指出户外花园比完全静音房间更放松、更少无聊。
central_sentences.keywords: quiet environment, more beneficial than totally silent
sentences: In a recent study, he found that participants reported more relaxation and less boredom when they sat quietly in an outdoor garden compared with a completely silent room.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: relatively quiet > totally silent -> outdoor garden vs silent room

central_sentences.sentence: In a recent study, he found that participants reported more relaxation and less boredom when they sat quietly in an outdoor garden compared with a completely silent room.

groups.title: Flotation Tanks
groups.desc:
Questions 18-21
Complete the summary below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
Write your answers in boxes 18-21 on your answer sheet.
groups.collect:
According to Justin Feinstein, flotation tanks allow people to concentrate on their own $40210$, which helps them relax and enables them to meditate.

Feinstein and his colleagues conducted an experiment in which 50 people, who were all suffering from stress and related issues, were given a $40211$ to complete before and after using a flotation tank. Participants reported a reduction in their symptoms after an hour in the tank, together with signs of relaxation and improved general $40212$.

In another experiment, the researchers had 48 people spend periods of 90 minutes either lying back in a chair or floating in a tank. Brain scans then revealed that those people who had been in a tank had decreased activity in parts of the brain associated with $40213$.

questions.analysis: 题干要求填写一个词，说明漂浮舱中帮助人们放松和冥想的关键。D段引用Justin Feinstein的话指出：‘正是专注于呼吸的能力帮助人们进入放松或冥想状态’。其中‘focus on the breath’与题干中的‘concentrate on their own...’形成同义替换，因此正确答案是‘breath’。

central_sentences.analysis: D段明确指出专注呼吸有助于进入放松或冥想状态。
sentences: But it is the ability to focus on the breath that helps people reach a relaxed or meditative state.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: focus on the breath -> concentrate on their own breath

central_sentences.sentence: But it is the ability to focus on the breath that helps people reach a relaxed or meditative state.

questions.analysis: 题干描述实验中50名压力相关症状的参与者在使用漂浮舱前后完成某项任务。E段明确提到Feinstein团队让参与者‘在漂浮前后回答一份问卷（answer a questionnaire prior to and following a flotation session）’。‘questionnaire’是原文原词，且与题干‘given a...to complete’逻辑一致，因此答案为‘questionnaire’。

central_sentences.analysis: E段提到参与者在漂浮前后填写问卷。
central_sentences.keywords: experiment, stress, complete before and after
sentences: Feinstein and his colleagues recruited 50 people with a variety of conditions related to stress and had them answer a questionnaire prior to and following a flotation session.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: answer a questionnaire -> given a questionnaire to complete

central_sentences.sentence: Feinstein and his colleagues recruited 50 people with a variety of conditions related to stress and had them answer a questionnaire prior to and following a flotation session.

questions.analysis: 题干要求填写一个词，表示参与者在漂浮后报告的整体改善。E段指出，参与者在一次一小时漂浮后，不仅肌肉紧张和疼痛减轻，还‘增加了放松感和整体幸福感（overall wellbeing）’。‘wellbeing’是原文用词，且‘improved general wellbeing’与‘increase in...overall wellbeing’意思一致，因此答案为‘wellbeing’。

central_sentences.analysis: E段指出漂浮后参与者报告整体幸福感提升。
sentences: Participants reported decreases in muscle tension, pain and symptoms of their conditions after a single, 1-hour float, alongside an increase in feelings of relaxation and overall wellbeing.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: overall wellbeing -> improved general wellbeing

central_sentences.sentence: Participants reported decreases in muscle tension, pain and symptoms of their conditions after a single, 1-hour float, alongside an increase in feelings of relaxation and overall wellbeing.

questions.analysis: 题干涉及脑扫描显示漂浮舱使用者大脑某些区域活动减少，这些区域与某种心理状态相关。E段明确说明：‘漂浮独特地降低了默认模式网络（DMN）的活动，而该脑区通常与抑郁症（depression）相关’。因此，空白处应填入‘depression’，准确反映原文中脑区与抑郁的关联。

central_sentences.analysis: E段说明漂浮降低与抑郁相关的默认模式网络活动。
central_sentences.keywords: brain scans, decreased activity, associated with depression
sentences: Float sessions uniquely decreased activity in the default mode network (DMN), a collection of brain regions commonly linked with depression.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: default mode network (DMN) linked with depression -> parts of brain associated with depression

central_sentences.sentence: Float sessions uniquely decreased activity in the default mode network (DMN), a collection of brain regions commonly linked with depression.

groups.desc:
Questions 22-26
Look at the following statements (Questions 22-26) and the list of people below.
Match each statement with the correct person, A, B, C or D.
Write the correct letter, A, B, C or D, in boxes 22-26 on your answer sheet.
NB You may use any letter more than once.

questions.title: It is unpleasant and upsetting for people to be placed in a silent environment against their will.
questions.analysis: 题干陈述‘违背意愿被置于寂静环境中会令人不快和痛苦’。F段中Tai Dotan Ben-Soussan（选项C）指出：‘当人们不想要寂静时，它可能会非常令人痛苦（very distressing）’。‘do not want silence’对应‘against their will’，‘distressing’对应‘unpleasant and upsetting’，因此答案为C。

central_sentences.analysis: Ben-Soussan（C）指出不愿沉默者会感到痛苦 distressing。
sentences: When people do not want silence, it can be very distressing.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: do not want silence -> placed in silent environment against their will

central_sentences.sentence: When people do not want silence, it can be very distressing.

questions.title: The trend towards creating quieter urban locations is likely to increase in the coming years.
questions.analysis: 题干称‘创造更安静城市环境的趋势未来可能增加’。C段末尾Nick Antonio（选项A）表示：‘我预计未来我们会看到更多此类举措（I expect we will see much more of this in the future）’。‘much more of this’指代前文所述的降噪措施，与题干中的‘trend...likely to increase’形成同义表达，因此答案为A。

central_sentences.analysis: Antonio（A）预测未来将有更多城市降噪举措。
sentences: I expect we will see much more of this in the future.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: I expect we will see much more of this -> trend likely to increase

central_sentences.sentence: I expect we will see much more of this in the future.

questions.title: When our body's senses are completely deprived of input, our minds compensate for this by creating the illusion of images and sounds.
questions.analysis: 题干描述‘当感官完全被剥夺输入时，大脑会生成声音和图像的幻觉’。D段中Justin Feinstein（选项B）解释：‘当你没有外部感官刺激时，大脑会试图填补空白，以理解这个黑暗寂静的世界’，并提到有人在舱内能‘听到眨眼的声音’或产生视觉效应。这与题干中‘mind compensates by creating illusions’完全对应，因此答案为B。

central_sentences.analysis: Feinstein（B）解释感官剥夺时大脑会生成声音和视觉效应。
sentences: When you do not have external sensory stimuli coming in, the brain tries to fill the void to make sense of this dark and silent world, he explains.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: brain tries to fill the void -> creates illusion of images and sounds

central_sentences.sentence: When you do not have external sensory stimuli coming in, the brain tries to fill the void to make sense of this dark and silent world, he explains.

questions.title: Even a short amount of silent time can have a positive impact.
questions.analysis: 题干指出‘即使短暂的沉默也能产生积极影响’。G段中Eric Pfeifer（选项D）认为：‘不需要花数小时沉默，每天几次几分钟的沉默可能比每周一次长时间沉默更好’。这直接支持‘short amount of silent time can have a positive impact’的观点，因此答案为D。

central_sentences.analysis: Pfeifer（D）认为短时高频沉默优于单次长时间沉默。
sentences: It is likely better to have more frequency of silence for a few minutes at a time than a longer period of silence only once a week.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: few minutes at a time -> short amount of silent time

central_sentences.sentence: It is likely better to have more frequency of silence for a few minutes at a time than a longer period of silence only once a week.

questions.title: External and internal quietness makes us more conscious of events occurring in our surroundings and helps us react appropriately to these events.
questions.analysis: 题干强调‘内外安静使人更觉察周围事件并作出恰当反应’。F段中Tai Dotan Ben-Soussan（选项C）表示：‘当我们找到安静的方式，不仅环境安静，内心也安静’，这‘使我们更能意识到周围发生的事及情境所需，从而做出更适当的回应’。该描述与题干完全吻合，因此答案为C。

central_sentences.analysis: Ben-Soussan（C）强调内外安静提升情境意识与恰当反应能力。
central_sentences.keywords: external and internal quietness, conscious, react appropriately
sentences: When we find ways to be quiet, we are not only quiet in our environment, but quiet in our inner selves, she explains.
This allows us to be more aware of what is happening around us and what the situation may require from us so we can provide a more adequate response.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: quiet in environment and inner selves -> external and internal quietness

central_sentences.sentence: When we find ways to be quiet, we are not only quiet in our environment, but quiet in our inner selves, she explains.
This allows us to be more aware of what is happening around us and what the situation may require from us so we can provide a more adequate response.

content.title: Book review: The World of Sugar by Ulbe Bosma

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Ulbe Bosma's The World of Sugar is a genuinely global history. Bosma discusses all the sugar-growing places of the world, beginning with Cuba and Java, the largest exporters of the early 20th century. But this is a history not just of cane sugar but also of beet sugar, an equally important form of traded sugar over the last hundred years. Beet sugar is grown mainly in Europe and the United States. It has also been massively subsidised and sold at artificially low prices on world markets, threatening the livelihood of producers of cane sugar.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Bosma's discussion of the sugar market in Britain gives a sense of the book's range. The sweet-toothed British first bought cane sugar from their own slavery-dependent colonial plantations. Following the abolition of slavery in the British Empire, cane sugar was imported to Britain from places which retained the practice, such as Cuba and Brazil. Towards the end of the 19th century, the British started to import beet sugar from continental Europe. Only in the 20th century was there a move to develop a national beet sugar industry.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: The book provides a global labour history, investigating the wide range of labour regimes associated with growing sugar. Contrary to popular belief, cane sugar production was never just restricted to large, dedicated plantations owned by rich men. For example, in Java, a huge exporter of sugar in the early 20th century, sugar cane was grown together with rice in an extraordinarily labour-intensive way by small farmers.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The World of Sugar is also a story of similarity and continuity in sugar cultivation. For example, imported labour has been used for much large-scale production. German beet fields employed Polish workers; Mexicans and many others, including Sicilians, were vital to US sugar production. Cane cutting, Bosma shows, remains a poorly paid and brutal business to this day in many places in the world. But as well as this, the book is about the continuity of the use of traditional methods on small farms. In the mid 20th-century, this type of sugar production dominated in South Asia and Latin America.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: This is also a history of capitalists and sugar dynasties, as well as corporations that in some cases have remained influential over very long periods. Great firms and great interests have had profound influence on the policies of states. In many places - not just the British Caribbean but Cuba and the Philippines too - a powerful sugar bourgeoisie played a major role in politics and their interests were consequently protected by trade barriers and subsidies. In the battle for control of the industry, it was inevitably the poor countries which came off worse. All this is explored by Bosma with wonderful subtlety and control.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: But sugar production was never just a matter of agriculture. It also involved the extraction, close to the place of harvest, of sucrose from the sugar plant, a process which required machinery powered by humans, animals, wind or steam. Further processes involved boiling and the separation of sugar from other materials in a process known as refinement. From very early on, sugar production was an energy-intensive industrial process, mostly taking place in the countryside and in refineries in centres of consumption, both small and large. The growth of the industry entailed a very rapid diffusion of ideas and techniques from one country to another. Cuba, for example, developed an extraordinarily dense system of railways to transport workers and cane, as well as steam-powered sugar factories. Particular varieties of cane sugar and beet sugar spread very rapidly across the world, in accordance with local needs and demands.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Where once only tiny quantities of sugar could be produced, now new techniques, varieties, fertilisers, irrigation systems and much more have turned gleaming white sugar into a ubiquitous chemical. Over the same time, there has been a massive increase in consumption. Once regarded as a luxury, sugar came to be promoted as a valuable source of energy. But as the consumption of sugar has increased, so has the harm it does, whether to people's teeth or weight. In the face of appalling obstruction from the sugar industry to attempts to reduce consumption, some countries have been forced to tax sugar in order to bring that about. The sugar industry has a history of attacking its critics and, when it comes to obesity, of trying to blame fats, and lack of exercise and self-control. And the recent past has seen worrying new developments in mass sweetening. High-fructose corn syrup made from maize using an enzymatic process invented in Japan in the 1960s has a similar number of calories to table sugar but is far cheaper to produce. It is now widely consumed, having been adopted in the making of soft drinks and a large number of processed foods, and is regarded as a leading cause of obesity.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: This is a wonderfully rich book, a model of global history, the history of production and the history of capitalism. Bosma avoids outbursts of emotion, celebratory or critical, even if they might have made his analysis of the multiple tragedies involving sugar all the more powerful. He shows that we could always have done without sugar and that today we have many alternative sources of sweetness. Yet many of the poorest people in the world still depend on it to make a living.

groups.desc:
Questions 27-30
Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
Write the correct letter in boxes 27-30 on your answer sheet.

questions.title: What does the reviewer suggest about the cultivation and trading of sugar in the first paragraph?

options.text: Sugar has played a major role in international relations.
Beet sugar has been made more internationally competitive.
Cane sugar is thought to be of superior quality to beet sugar.
New locations for cultivating sugar have increased production.

questions.analysis: 题干询问第一段中评论者对糖的种植与贸易的看法。原文指出甜菜糖在欧洲和美国被‘大量补贴’并在国际市场上‘以人为压低的价格出售’，这使其更具国际竞争力，直接对应选项B中的‘Beet sugar has been made more internationally competitive’。其他选项未在该段得到支持。

central_sentences.reason: 原文明确指出甜菜糖被大量补贴并在国际市场低价销售，对应选项B。
central_sentences.analysis: 甜菜糖因补贴而更具国际竞争力。
central_sentences.keywords: beet sugar, subsidised, artificially low prices
sentences: Beet sugar is grown mainly in Europe and the United States.
It has also been massively subsidised and sold at artificially low prices on world markets, threatening the livelihood of producers of cane sugar.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: made more internationally competitive → subsidised and sold at artificially low prices

central_sentences.sentence: Beet sugar is grown mainly in Europe and the United States.
It has also been massively subsidised and sold at artificially low prices on world markets, threatening the livelihood of producers of cane sugar.

questions.title: In the second paragraph, when discussing the sugar market in Britain, the reviewer stresses

options.text: how the sources used changed over time.
how developments in agriculture affected trade.
the increased demand for sugar over the years.
the growing support for ethical methods of cultivation.

questions.analysis: 第二段按时间顺序描述了英国糖来源的变化：最初来自本国殖民地种植园，废除奴隶制后转从古巴、巴西进口甘蔗糖，19世纪末开始进口欧洲甜菜糖，20世纪才发展本国甜菜糖产业。这种清晰的时间线表明评论者强调的是‘糖来源随时间变化’，对应选项A。

central_sentences.reason: 段落清晰按时间顺序描述糖来源变化，对应选项A。
central_sentences.analysis: 英国糖来源随时间从殖民地转向他国再发展本国产业。
sentences: The sweet-toothed British first bought cane sugar from their own slavery-dependent colonial plantations.
Following the abolition of slavery in the British Empire, cane sugar was imported to Britain from places which retained the practice, such as Cuba and Brazil.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: sources used changed over time → colonial plantations → Cuba/Brazil → continental Europe → national industry

central_sentences.sentence: The sweet-toothed British first bought cane sugar from their own slavery-dependent colonial plantations.
Following the abolition of slavery in the British Empire, cane sugar was imported to Britain from places which retained the practice, such as Cuba and Brazil.

questions.title: What is the reviewer doing in the third paragraph?

options.text: describing an efficient approach to sugar cultivation
explaining why the use of sugar plantations declined
addressing a misconception about the growing of sugar cane
evaluating different approaches to the cultivation of sugar cane

questions.analysis: 第三段开头明确使用‘Contrary to popular belief’（与普遍看法相反）来纠正一个误解，即甘蔗生产并非仅限于富人拥有的大型种植园。作者以爪哇小农为例说明甘蔗也可由小农户种植。因此，评论者是在‘addressing a misconception about the growing of sugar cane’，对应选项C。

central_sentences.reason: 明确使用“contrary to popular belief”指出常见误解，对应选项C。
central_sentences.analysis: 作者纠正了甘蔗仅由大种植园生产的误解。
central_sentences.keywords: contrary to popular belief, never just restricted to large plantations
sentences: Contrary to popular belief, cane sugar production was never just restricted to large, dedicated plantations owned by rich men.
For example, in Java, a huge exporter of sugar in the early 20th century, sugar cane was grown together with rice in an extraordinarily labour-intensive way by small farmers.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: addressing a misconception → contrary to popular belief

central_sentences.sentence: Contrary to popular belief, cane sugar production was never just restricted to large, dedicated plantations owned by rich men.
For example, in Java, a huge exporter of sugar in the early 20th century, sugar cane was grown together with rice in an extraordinarily labour-intensive way by small farmers.

questions.title: In the final paragraph, what does the reviewer suggest is the overall message of Bosma's book?

options.text: Sugar is a harmful and unnecessary product.
Economic pressure is needed to control sugar production.
Conditions for workers in sugar production should be improved.
Intensive marketing of sugar has had disastrous consequences.

questions.analysis: 最后一段指出‘He shows that we could always have done without sugar’，说明作者认为糖并非必需品，结合前文提到糖对健康有害（如导致肥胖、蛀牙），可推断整体信息是糖既无必要又有害，对应选项A。虽然末句提到穷人依赖糖谋生，但这并不否定其‘unnecessary’的本质。

central_sentences.reason: 末段明确指出人类本可不需要糖，说明其非必要，且前文提及其危害，支持A。
central_sentences.analysis: 作者认为糖非必需且有害，呼应选项A。
central_sentences.keywords: could always have done without sugar, unnecessary
sentences: He shows that we could always have done without sugar and that today we have many alternative sources of sweetness.
Yet many of the poorest people in the world still depend on it to make a living.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: unnecessary → could always have done without sugar

central_sentences.sentence: He shows that we could always have done without sugar and that today we have many alternative sources of sweetness.
Yet many of the poorest people in the world still depend on it to make a living.

groups.title: Sugar cultivation and production
groups.desc:
Questions 31-36
Complete the summary using the list of words, A-I, below.
Write the correct letter, A-I, in boxes 31-36 on your answer sheet.
groups.collect: The book The World of Sugar points out the similarities in the way that sugar was cultivated around the world. In the big industries in both Germany and the US, sugar farming depended on $40223$. However, in other parts of the world such as South Asia and Latin America, $40224$ continued. Sugar production has also involved $40225$ who were eager to protect their markets. In countries such as Cuba the sugar industry therefore had a major influence on $40226$. To support the interests of sugar producers, $40227$ were established. As a result of this, $40228$ were penalised.

questions.analysis: 题干要求填入德国和美国糖业所依赖的劳动力类型。原文第四段提到‘German beet fields employed Polish workers；Mexicans and many others, including Sicilians, were vital to US sugar production’，这些都属于移民工人，对应选项H（migrant workers）。

central_sentences.reason: 原文列举多国移民工人，匹配选项H（migrant workers）。
central_sentences.analysis: 德美糖业依赖移民劳工，对应H。
central_sentences.keywords: German beet fields, Polish workers; US sugar production, Mexicans, Sicilians
sentences: German beet fields employed Polish workers; Mexicans and many others, including Sicilians, were vital to US sugar production.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: migrant workers → Polish workers, Mexicans, Sicilians

central_sentences.sentence: German beet fields employed Polish workers; Mexicans and many others, including Sicilians, were vital to US sugar production.

questions.analysis: 题干指出在南亚和拉丁美洲持续进行的生产方式。原文第四段提到‘the continuity of the use of traditional methods on small farms’以及‘this type of sugar production dominated in South Asia and Latin America’，说明小规模种植长期存在，对应选项E（small-scale cultivation）。

central_sentences.reason: “small farms”和“this type of production”指小规模种植，对应E。
central_sentences.analysis: 亚拉地区以小农传统方式持续生产，对应E。
central_sentences.keywords: continuity of traditional methods on small farms, South Asia and Latin America
sentences: But as well as this, the book is about the continuity of the use of traditional methods on small farms.
In the mid 20th-century, this type of sugar production dominated in South Asia and Latin America.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: small-scale cultivation → traditional methods on small farms

central_sentences.sentence: But as well as this, the book is about the continuity of the use of traditional methods on small farms.
In the mid 20th-century, this type of sugar production dominated in South Asia and Latin America.

questions.analysis: 题干要求填入试图保护市场利益的群体。第五段提到‘capitalists and sugar dynasties’、‘great firms’以及‘a powerful sugar bourgeoisie’，这些都指有权势的个人和企业，他们通过影响政策保护自身利益，对应选项I（powerful individuals and businesses）。

central_sentences.reason: “capitalists”, “dynasties”, “bourgeoisie”对应I（powerful individuals and businesses）。
central_sentences.analysis: 糖业巨头为保护市场利益而行动，对应I。
central_sentences.keywords: capitalists, sugar dynasties, corporations, powerful sugar bourgeoisie
sentences: This is also a history of capitalists and sugar dynasties, as well as corporations that in some cases have remained influential over very long periods.

In many places - not just the British Caribbean but Cuba and the Philippines too - a powerful sugar bourgeoisie played a major role in politics and their interests were consequently protected by trade barriers and subsidies.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: powerful individuals and businesses → sugar bourgeoisie, great firms

central_sentences.sentence: This is also a history of capitalists and sugar dynasties, as well as corporations that in some cases have remained influential over very long periods.

In many places - not just the British Caribbean but Cuba and the Philippines too - a powerful sugar bourgeoisie played a major role in politics and their interests were consequently protected by trade barriers and subsidies.

questions.analysis: 题干问糖业在古巴等国对什么产生了重大影响。原文第五段指出‘a powerful sugar bourgeoisie played a major role in politics’，即糖业精英深度参与政治，这意味着他们对国家政府有重大影响，对应选项A（national governments）。

central_sentences.reason: “played a major role in politics”即影响国家政府，对应A。
central_sentences.analysis: 糖业精英影响国家政治，对应A。
central_sentences.keywords: sugar bourgeoisie played a major role in politics
sentences: In many places - not just the British Caribbean but Cuba and the Philippines too - a powerful sugar bourgeoisie played a major role in politics and their interests were consequently protected by trade barriers and subsidies.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: influence on national governments → played a major role in politics

central_sentences.sentence: In many places - not just the British Caribbean but Cuba and the Philippines too - a powerful sugar bourgeoisie played a major role in politics and their interests were consequently protected by trade barriers and subsidies.

questions.analysis: 题干要求填入为保护糖业利益而设立的措施。第五段提到糖业利益‘were consequently protected by trade barriers and subsidies’，贸易壁垒和补贴属于财政控制手段，对应选项G（financial controls）。

central_sentences.reason: 贸易壁垒和补贴属于财政控制手段，匹配G。
central_sentences.analysis: 为保护利益设立贸易壁垒和补贴，属财政控制，对应G。
sentences: their interests were consequently protected by trade barriers and subsidies.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: financial controls → subsidies, trade barriers

central_sentences.sentence: their interests were consequently protected by trade barriers and subsidies.

questions.analysis: 题干指出某些国家因此受到惩罚。第五段末句明确说‘In the battle for control of the industry, it was inevitably the poor countries which came off worse’，即较贫穷国家在竞争中处于劣势，对应选项C（less wealthy nations）。

central_sentences.reason: “poor countries”直接对应C（less wealthy nations）。
central_sentences.analysis: 穷国在产业争夺中受损，对应C。
central_sentences.keywords: poor countries which came off worse
sentences: In the battle for control of the industry, it was inevitably the poor countries which came off worse.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: less wealthy nations → poor countries

central_sentences.sentence: In the battle for control of the industry, it was inevitably the poor countries which came off worse.

groups.desc:
Questions 37-40
Do the following statements agree with the views of the writer in Reading Passage 3?
In boxes 37-40 on your answer sheet, write
YES if the statement agrees with the views of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the views of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

questions.title: Sugar has now become available in large quantities due to a range of agricultural developments.
questions.analysis: 题干陈述糖因农业发展而大量生产。第七段开头指出‘new techniques, varieties, fertilisers, irrigation systems and much more have turned... sugar into a ubiquitous chemical’，说明产量激增确实归功于一系列农业技术进步，因此答案为YES。

central_sentences.reason: 原文明确将产量激增归因于农业技术发展，支持YES。
central_sentences.analysis: 农业技术进步使糖产量大增，陈述正确。
central_sentences.keywords: new techniques, varieties, fertilisers, irrigation systems, turned sugar into ubiquitous chemical
sentences: Where once only tiny quantities of sugar could be produced, now new techniques, varieties, fertilisers, irrigation systems and much more have turned gleaming white sugar into a ubiquitous chemical.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: large quantities due to agricultural developments → new techniques... turned sugar ubiquitous

central_sentences.sentence: Where once only tiny quantities of sugar could be produced, now new techniques, varieties, fertilisers, irrigation systems and much more have turned gleaming white sugar into a ubiquitous chemical.

questions.title: Advertisers initially marketed sugar as a luxury product.
questions.analysis: 题干称广告商最初将糖作为奢侈品营销。原文虽提到糖‘once regarded as a luxury’，但并未说明这是广告商的营销策略，也未提及‘advertisers’或相关行为，因此无法判断作者是否认同此观点，答案为NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.reason: “regarded as”不等于“advertised as”，未提及广告行为，故NOT GIVEN。
central_sentences.analysis: 原文说糖曾被视为奢侈品，但未提广告商营销策略，无法判断。
central_sentences.keywords: once regarded as a luxury, promoted as energy source
sentences: Once regarded as a luxury, sugar came to be promoted as a valuable source of energy.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: marketed as luxury → regarded as a luxury

central_sentences.sentence: Once regarded as a luxury, sugar came to be promoted as a valuable source of energy.

questions.title: The invention of high-fructose corn syrup was a positive development.
questions.analysis: 题干称高果糖玉米糖浆的发明是积极发展。但原文第七段将其描述为‘worrying new developments’并指出它是‘a leading cause of obesity’，明显持负面态度，因此该陈述与作者观点矛盾，答案为NO。

central_sentences.reason: 称其为“worrying”且致肥胖主因，明显否定，故NO。
central_sentences.analysis: 作者视高果糖玉米糖浆为负面发展，陈述错误。
central_sentences.keywords: high-fructose corn syrup, leading cause of obesity, worrying new developments
sentences: And the recent past has seen worrying new developments in mass sweetening.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: positive development → worrying new developments, leading cause of obesity

central_sentences.sentence: And the recent past has seen worrying new developments in mass sweetening.

questions.title: High-fructose corn syrup is an ingredient in many processed foods.
questions.analysis: 题干称高果糖玉米糖浆是许多加工食品的成分。原文第七段明确说它‘has been adopted in the making of soft drinks and a large number of processed foods’，直接支持该陈述，因此答案为YES。

central_sentences.analysis: 高果糖浆广泛用于加工食品
sentences: It is now widely consumed, having been adopted in the making of soft drinks and a large number of processed foods, and is regarded as a leading cause of obesity.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: ingredient in many processed foods -> adopted in... a large number of processed foods

central_sentences.sentence: It is now widely consumed, having been adopted in the making of soft drinks and a large number of processed foods, and is regarded as a leading cause of obesity.
